title: AI repo integration plan for Luke

goals.title: Freeze integration baseline
goals.objective: Capture the current state of reference clones, Luke git status, and existing Radar, memory, dashboard, and Katbot integration seams before implementation.
goals.rationale: Luke already has active dirty work and working Radar/Katbot/UI paths; integration should start from exact evidence.
scope: Record `git status --short --branch`.
Verify `tools/reference-repos/` contains the ten selected shallow clones.
Record the current Radar routes in `agents/agent-00-brain.js`.
Record the current UI surfaces: `radar-dashboard.html`, `luke-shell.html`, `brain-dashboard.html`, `operator-v2.html`, `trading-window.html`.
Record the current Katbot capture and broadcast seams in `agents/agent-14-kat.js`.
non_goals: No implementation.
No dependency installation.
No trading-path edits.
goals.stop_condition: A baseline section exists in the implementation report with exact paths and current behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Reference clone list and commits are recorded.
Radar, memory, UI, and Katbot seam files are listed.
Existing user-owned dirty files are explicitly left untouched.
risks: A dirty worktree can hide unrelated user changes.
assumptions: The existing dirty trading/NinjaTrader work remains user-owned.

goals.title: Add reference repo registry
goals.objective: Create a Luke-native registry describing each external reference repo, its local path, upstream URL, commit, category, and approved Luke use.
goals.rationale: A registry prevents the cloned repos from becoming unmanaged mystery folders or accidental runtime dependencies.
scope: Add `config/ai-reference-repos.json` or equivalent.
Add a small reader module such as `lib/reference-repos/registry.js`.
Include local path, upstream URL, checked-out commit, license when available, category, allowed surfaces, and banned surfaces.
Add tests for registry loading and missing-path behavior.
non_goals: Do not import upstream repo source into Luke runtime.
Do not fetch network updates automatically.
goals.stop_condition: Luke can load and summarize the reference repo registry without scanning arbitrary files.
acceptance_criteria: The registry names all ten repos.
Each repo has allowed and disallowed Luke surfaces.
Missing clone directories degrade to a clear warning, not a crash.
risks: Over-modeling the registry could add unnecessary abstraction.
assumptions: A simple JSON registry is enough for the first pass.

goals.title: Add agent and coder guidance
goals.objective: Integrate the useful guidance patterns from Karpathy, Matt Pocock, and selected SuperClaude material into concise Luke-specific instructions.
goals.rationale: The user wants collaborative/helpful behavior and better coder workflow without importing a large framework.
scope: Create or update `AGENTS.md`.
Add Luke-specific engineering rules for small verified steps, explicit assumptions, helpful pushback, and dirty-worktree protection.
Add shared-language guidance in `docs/LUKE_SHARED_LANGUAGE.md` only if it reduces future ambiguity.
Preserve sacred trading smoke paths and human-gated trading rules.
non_goals: Do not copy full upstream instruction files wholesale.
Do not make simple tasks ceremony-heavy.
goals.stop_condition: Luke has concise repo-local guidance that future Codex sessions can follow.
acceptance_criteria: `AGENTS.md` is short, Luke-specific, and references external repos only as inspiration.
Guidance includes friendly collaboration, concrete pushback, and verifiable completion.
Guidance preserves `/status`, `/balance`, `/saty`, `/ready`, and `/alert`.
risks: Too much guidance can slow small fixes.
assumptions: The repo currently needs concise operational rules more than a full framework.

goals.title: Add Radar reference-review lane
goals.objective: Make Radar the approval surface for reference-derived ideas before they become implementation work.
goals.rationale: Radar already has source typing, review states, source health, item detail, and dashboard routes.
scope: Add source types or themes for reference repos and integration ideas.
Add a script or module that turns selected registry entries into reviewable Radar items.
Add accept/reject/defer guidance so rejected ideas remain durable evidence.
Update `radar-dashboard.html` only as much as needed to filter or label reference ideas.
non_goals: Do not turn Radar into a bookmark manager.
Do not ingest every upstream file.
goals.stop_condition: A small set of reference-derived integration ideas can be reviewed in Radar.
acceptance_criteria: Radar can distinguish reference ideas from manual pastes and trading notes.
Accepted, contradicted, and archived states work for reference ideas.
Daily/Radar brief does not treat reference ideas as trading authority.
risks: Too many reference cards could bury real operator Radar items.
assumptions: Only actionable reference ideas should enter Radar.

goals.title: Improve memory and context
goals.objective: Borrow MemPalace and Hermes memory concepts to add scoped recall and relationships without replacing Luke's existing storage.
goals.rationale: Luke already has companion memory and context bins, but needs better source scoping and recall reasons.
scope: Add optional project/person/topic scopes to memory entries.
Add recall reason metadata to memory search results.
Add relationships between companion memory entries and Radar items.
Update `docs/LUKE_COMPANION_MEMORY.md` with current storage and boundaries.
non_goals: Do not install MemPalace into Luke runtime yet.
Do not migrate memory storage in the first pass.
goals.stop_condition: Memory recall is more explainable and cross-linked to Radar while retaining current storage compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: Existing memory tests still pass.
Old memory entries still load.
New scoped entries include safe defaults.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/companion-memory*.test.js tests/luke-context-bins*.test.js
risks: Memory schema changes can strand older entries if compatibility is not preserved.
assumptions: JSON plus JSONL remain the first-pass storage model.

goals.title: Bridge Katbot into Radar and memory
goals.objective: Route selected Katbot captures, vision records, and confluence alerts into Radar and memory context as reviewable evidence.
goals.rationale: Katbot already captures Discord content and broadcasts context, but Radar can provide durable review state.
scope: Add a narrow bridge from Katbot capture/confluence paths to Radar ingest.
Link Katbot Radar items back to message ids, source labels, and vision ids where available.
Expose accepted/rejected Katbot context to memory and operator readouts.
Keep all Katbot output read-only and human-gated.
non_goals: Do not let Katbot create live candidates.
Do not post extra Discord messages.
Do not expand live vision model use without explicit approval.
goals.stop_condition: Katbot context has a review trail in Radar without changing execution behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Katbot bridge is gated and deduped.
Radar items include provenance and review state.
Operator/trading windows still show read-only no-execution status.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/*kat*.test.js tests/*radar*.test.js
risks: Katbot can generate high-volume events; bridge must avoid Radar spam.
assumptions: Only selected or summarized Katbot events should become Radar items.

goals.title: Add research-loop metadata
goals.objective: Apply Qlib and autoresearch patterns to Luke research scripts through metadata, export formats, and keep/discard reports.
goals.rationale: Luke has many research and backtest scripts; they need stronger experiment discipline before more strategy work.
scope: Define a lightweight research run metadata format.
Record hypothesis, input data window, commit, command, result, and accept/reject decision.
Add optional export shape for later Qlib-compatible analysis.
Apply first to one bounded research lane before broad rollout.
non_goals: Do not install Qlib as a runtime dependency.
Do not run unattended overnight code-edit loops.
Do not promote research output to live trading.
goals.stop_condition: One Luke research lane emits auditable experiment metadata and a keep/discard summary.
acceptance_criteria: Generated research output stays under `artifacts/research/` unless promoted.
Reports distinguish hypothesis from proven result.
Trading UI remains read-only.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/*research*.test.js tests/*backtest*.test.js
risks: Research reports can look authoritative even when based on narrow samples.
assumptions: Metadata discipline is higher leverage than adopting a Python quant platform immediately.

goals.title: Expose integration status in UI
goals.objective: Add restrained dashboard visibility for approved reference integration status after Radar proves useful.
goals.rationale: The dashboard should show useful operating state, not hide new intelligence in files.
scope: Start with Radar labels or filters.
Add a small shell/brain status line only if the Radar lane is useful.
Show trading research status in operator/trading windows only when relevant and read-only.
Avoid decorative redesign.
non_goals: No marketing page.
No large visual redesign.
No trading execution controls.
goals.stop_condition: The UI exposes integration status without cluttering core daily/trading workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Radar displays reference idea status clearly.
Shell/brain additions are optional and compact.
Operator/trading windows remain read-only.
validation: cmd /c npx vitest run tests/brain-dashboard.test.js tests/operator-v2-ui.test.js tests/trading-ui-readonly.test.js
risks: Dashboard clutter would make Luke less useful during trading.
assumptions: Radar is the primary review surface; dashboards are status surfaces.

goals.title: Verify and report decisions
goals.objective: Run focused verification and produce a final integration decision report covering accepted, rejected, deferred, and risky items.
goals.rationale: The user wants a plan they can push back on and later a trustworthy implementation record.
scope: Run focused syntax checks and relevant tests.
Run Radar, memory, operator, and trading-window proof scripts when touched.
Document skipped checks and exact reasons.
Update the report with accepted, rejected, deferred, and next recommended slices.
non_goals: Do not hide failed or skipped checks.
Do not claim live readiness.
goals.stop_condition: The final report is sufficient for the user to approve, reject, or redirect the integration.
acceptance_criteria: Exact commands and outcomes are recorded.
Residual risks are listed.
No unreviewed external code is wired into Luke runtime.
risks: Full verification may be blocked by current dirty work or environment-specific services.
assumptions: Implementation should pause after goals 1-4 unless the user explicitly approves deeper memory/Katbot/research changes.
